descript.abstract: This polygon shapefile contains 2005 poverty data for 855 rural subcounties in Uganda.
descript.supplinf:
No data (all urban areas, 2002 Kotido district because census data were not deemed reliable; 6 rural Subcounties without data that were dropped in the econometric model) by putting "NHHLDS" <>0 AND "DNAME_2002" <> 'KOTIDO' in Definition Query.
The poverty indicators produced by the Uganda Bureau of Statistics (UBOS) are based on household consumption expenditures, including both food and a range of nonfood items such as education, transport, health, and rent. Households are defined as poor when their total expenditures fall below Uganda's rural or urban national poverty lines. These lines equate to a basket of goods and services that meets basic monthly requirements (UBOS and ILRI, 2007). In 2005, the national poverty line (an average of the poverty lines in Uganda?s four regions) was 20,789 Uganda Shillings (US$ 12) per month in rural areas and 22,175 Uganda Shillings (US$ 13) per month in urban settings.
UGANDA BUREAU OF STATISTICS (UBOS) AND INTERNATIONAL LIVESTOCK RESEARCH INSTITUTE (ILRI). 2008. 2005 Poverty GIS Database. Kampala, Uganda: Government of Uganda, UBOS and ILRI.

idinfo.native: Microsoft Windows XP Version 5.1 (Build 2600) Service Pack 3; ESRI ArcCatalog 9.3.1.3000
idinfo.datacred: Uganda Bureau of Statistics (UBOS) and International Livestock Research Institute (ILRI). (2008) 2005 Poverty GIS Database. Kampala, Uganda: Government of Uganda, UBOS and ILRI.
idinfo.useconst: Dataset is not for use in litigation. While efforts have been made to ensure that these data are accurate and reliable within the state of the art, WRI cannot assume liability for any damages, or misrepresentations, caused by any inaccuracies in the data, or as a result of the data to be used on a particular system. WRI makes no warranty, expressed or implied, nor does the fact of distribution constitute such a warranty.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Total number of households in subcounty
Total number of people in subcounty
Poverty gap (as a percent of the poverty line)
Settled area where open water bodies, and National Parks and Wildlife Reserves over 50,000 ha were excluded (square km)
Total number of poor in subcounty
Poverty density in subcounty (number of poor people per square km)
Population density in subcounty (number of people per square km)

cntorgp.cntorg: REQUIRED: The organization responsible for the metadata information.
cntorgp.cntper: REQUIRED: The person responsible for the metadata information.

cntaddr.addrtype: REQUIRED: The mailing and/or physical address for the organization or individual.
cntaddr.city: REQUIRED: The city of the address.
cntaddr.state: REQUIRED: The state or province of the address.
cntaddr.postal: REQUIRED: The ZIP or other postal code of the address.

cntinfo.cntvoice: REQUIRED: The telephone number by which individuals can speak to the organization or individual.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standards for Digital Geospatial Metadata

dataIdInfo.envirDesc: Microsoft Windows 7 Version 6.1 (Build 7601) Service Pack 1; Esri ArcGIS 10.2.2.3552

idCitation.otherCitDet: Data originally accessed from Humanitarian Response Common Operational Dataset Registry: https://cod.humanitarianresponse.info/fr/node/4413. 05/25/2014.

dataIdInfo.idAbs: This polygon shapefile contains 2005 poverty data for 855 rural subcounties in Uganda.
dataIdInfo.idCredit: Uganda Bureau of Statistics and International Livestock Research Institute (ILRI). (2008). 2005 Poverty GIS Database. Kampala, Uganda: Government of Uganda, UBOS and ILRI.
dataIdInfo.idPurp: These data are intended for researchers, students, policy makers and the general public for reference and mapping purposes, and may be used for basic applications such as viewing, querying, and map output production.

LegConsts.useLimit: No restrictions on access. Data should not be used in litigation.

catCitation.resTitle: Feature Catalog for 2005 Rural Poverty GIS Database: Uganda
